rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for all AGREE content, with type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL features or previously declared helpers.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only paths.
Validate AGREE scalar types match AADL expectations: int, real, bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Use eq for equation declarations, const for constants, assume/guarantee for assertions, and assign for implementation bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or use subcomponent ports in type contracts.
Avoid referencing AADL properties (e.g., Deadline, Period) directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in requirement context
